summary: Linked the stage-related item unit `melee/it/items/itoctarock` by moving it from NonMatching to Matching and defining its missing `.data` item state table in `itoctarock.c`. The PR also corrected the declaration of `it_804D6D40` from `f32**` to `f32*`, allowing direct array indexing and eliminating a cast. decomp.dev reported one new `.data` match for `main/melee/it/items/itoctarock`, improving linked code by 4224 bytes and matching the unit's `.data` from 0.00% to 100.00%.
what_changed: `src/melee/it/items/itoctarock.c` added the global `ItemStateTable it_803F8B48[]` with nine entries for `itOctarock_UnkMotion0` through `itOctarock_UnkMotion8` animation, physics, and collision callbacks.
The state table includes a `NULL` collision callback for `itOctarock_UnkMotion5_Coll`, matching the data layout rather than forcing a nonexistent function pointer.
The extern declaration for `it_804D6D40` changed from `extern f32** it_804D6D40;` to `extern f32* it_804D6D40;`.
The random branch threshold access in `it_802E4B00` changed from `((f32*) it_804D6D40)[2]` to `it_804D6D40[2]` after correcting the extern type.
smart_moves: Focused the link fix on the missing `.data` object instead of rewriting code paths, which was enough to make the unit link as Matching.
Recovered the `ItemStateTable` as a concrete C data definition with exact callback ordering and state flags, producing a 100% `.data` match for the unit.
Corrected the type of `it_804D6D40` so the code expresses the real data shape and no longer needs a local cast at use sites.
Preserved unknown motion names such as `itOctarock_UnkMotion0_Anim` rather than over-naming behavior without evidence.
decomp_lessons: For item units stuck as NonMatching, check whether missing `ItemStateTable` data is the blocker before attempting larger code changes.
A unit can become linkable by adding accurate global data definitions even when many functions retain placeholder or unknown names.
Extern pointer depth matters for matching and readability: if a table is used as a float array, declare it as `f32*` and index directly instead of casting from an incorrect type.
When an item state has no callback for a slot, represent that slot as `NULL` in the state table rather than inventing a symbol.
After restoring data, remember to update `configure.py` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` for the object.
naming_conventions: Item state callbacks use the pattern `itOctarock_UnkMotionN_Anim`, `itOctarock_UnkMotionN_Phys`, and `itOctarock_UnkMotionN_Coll` while behavior remains unidentified.
The item file follows the lowercase item source naming convention `itoctarock.c`.
Unidentified globals retained address-style names such as `it_803F8B48` and `it_804D6D40`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Define the exact `ItemStateTable` entries in C to match `.data`, including integer state IDs and callback pointer order.
Use `NULL` for absent table callbacks to preserve the binary data layout.
Fix incorrect extern types to remove casts and better align generated code/data references with the original object.
Promote the object to `Object(Matching, ...)` only after the unit's code/data can link.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed one new match: `main/melee/it/items/itoctarock` `.data` improved from 0.00% to 100.00%, adding 144 matched data bytes.
Automated metrics also reported linked code +4224 bytes, matched data +144 bytes, and linked data +192 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find other item units marked NonMatching that are missing an `ItemStateTable` definition.
Search for incorrect extern declarations using casts like `((f32*) symbol)[index]` that may hide pointer-depth mistakes.
Compare other stage-related item state tables for callback ordering and `NULL` callback slots.
Investigate whether `it_803F8B48` and `it_804D6D40` can be given semantic names once Octarock behavior is better understood.
